meaning: Working from a location outside the traditional office, often from home
example: Remote work has become the norm for many tech companies.

meaning: Communication that does not require all parties to be available at the same time
example: Asynchronous communication allows global teams to collaborate across time zones.

meaning: Communication that occurs in real time with all parties present simultaneously
example: Video calls are a form of synchronous communication.

meaning: Online storage of data accessible from any device with internet access
example: The team stores all project files on cloud storage for easy access.

meaning: A file that multiple users can view and edit simultaneously online
example: They used a shared document to co-write the project report.

meaning: A system that tracks changes to files so previous versions can be restored
example: Version control ensures no edits are permanently lost.

meaning: Software designed to help teams work together efficiently online
example: Slack and Trello are popular collaboration tools for remote teams.

meaning: A virtual environment where employees access work tools and communicate online
example: The company set up a digital workspace for its distributed team.

meaning: A brief daily check-in meeting where team members share progress and blockers
example: The team holds a 15-minute stand-up meeting every morning.

meaning: The process of tracking, organising, and prioritising work tasks
example: Effective task management keeps the remote team aligned on priorities.

meaning: The latest time by which a task or project must be completed
example: All team members must submit their sections before the deadline.

meaning: A key point or significant achievement in a project's timeline
example: Completing the prototype was an important project milestone.

meaning: A defined sequence of steps or processes followed to complete a task
example: An efficient workflow reduces time wasted on repetitive steps.

meaning: A project management approach based on iterative progress and flexibility
example: The development team follows an agile methodology with two-week sprints.

meaning: A short, time-boxed period in which a team completes a set amount of work
example: The team plans new features at the start of each sprint.

meaning: Virtual Private Network; a secure connection to a private network over the internet
example: Employees connect via VPN to access internal company systems remotely.

meaning: A region of the globe observing a uniform standard time
example: Schedule meetings mindful of everyone's time zone.

meaning: The times during which a person is reachable and able to communicate
example: Share your availability on the shared calendar so the team can plan meetings.

meaning: A process where responses to actions are used to improve future performance
example: Regular check-ins create a healthy feedback loop within the team.

meaning: A work model combining in-office and remote working
example: The company adopted a hybrid work policy allowing two remote days per week.

meaning: A specific task assigned to a person following a meeting or discussion
example: Each action item was assigned an owner and a due date.

meaning: A brief meeting or conversation to align team members on status or decisions
example: Let's have a quick sync before the client call.

meaning: The process of integrating a new employee or user into a system or organisation
example: Virtual onboarding helps remote employees feel welcomed from day one.

meaning: A centralised repository of information and documentation for a team or organisation
example: New employees can find answers in the company knowledge base.

meaning: To refer a problem to a higher authority or more experienced person
example: If the issue is not resolved today, we will need to escalate it.

meaning: A person or group with an interest in the outcome of a project or decision
example: Keep all stakeholders informed of project progress via weekly updates.

meaning: A point of contact or interaction between team members or with a client
example: Regular touchpoints help maintain strong client relationships.

meaning: A person's capacity to take on more work or responsibilities
example: I don't have the bandwidth to take on another project this week.

meaning: An obstacle preventing progress on a task or project
example: The delayed approval is a blocker for the entire team.

meaning: A meeting held after a project or sprint to reflect on what went well and what to improve
example: The team's retrospective revealed a need for clearer communication.

meaning: A brief message communicating current progress or situation
example: She posted a status update in the project channel every Friday.

meaning: Written materials that record processes, decisions, or information
example: Good documentation allows team members to work independently.

meaning: Involving members from different departments or specialisations
example: The cross-functional team included developers, designers, and marketers.

meaning: The connection of different software tools so they work together seamlessly
example: The Slack and Google Calendar integration sends meeting reminders automatically.

meaning: A visual interface displaying key information and metrics at a glance
example: The project dashboard shows task completion rates in real time.

meaning: Working together on shared content simultaneously over the internet
example: Real-time collaboration tools allow multiple editors to update a document at once.

meaning: To transfer a file from a local device to the internet or a shared system
example: Please upload the final version of the report to the shared drive.

meaning: To transfer a file from the internet or a shared system to a local device
example: Download the meeting recording from the link in your email.

meaning: Access rights granted to a user to view or edit a file or system
example: You need edit permission to make changes to the shared spreadsheet.

meaning: An organisation's dedicated area within a collaboration or messaging platform
example: All company communications happen in our Slack workspace.

meaning: Software or services hosted on remote internet servers rather than locally
example: The company switched to a cloud-based communication platform.
